title: staff — Eloquent observers
description: Two observers enforce the module's write-time invariants: employment lifecycle (StaffObserver — cross-tenant defense, one-active-Staff-per-user-tenant, one-active-branch-manager-per-branch, compensation-write-permission, cascade-check-on-offboard), and coach lifecycle (CoachObserver — parent-staff-must-be-active, one-Coach-per-Staff, certification-shape-validation, rating-write-protection). The compensation write path additionally consults the CompensationGate binding — the observer only knows *whether* the caller carries the permission; the gate encapsulates *how* the check runs (permission + optional secondary-approval flow for large compensation deltas per staff.compensation.max_delta_percent config).

handles: creating → populate ULID prefix `stf_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `staff` feature (all tiers have this, but the check exists for defense-in-depth when a feature-flag kill-switch is flipped)
creating → refuse when user.tenant_id != tenant_id (cross-tenant defense-in-depth; the compliance-auditor catches schema-level bad FKs, this catches a bad write)
creating → refuse when parent Branch.status != 'active' (returns `STAFF_BRANCH_NOT_ACTIVE`)

creating → refuse when user_id already has an active (deleted_at IS NULL) Staff row in this tenant via the composite unique (tenant_id, user_id, deleted_at IS NULL) partial index; return `STAFF_USER_ALREADY_HAS_ACTIVE_ROW`
creating → refuse when employee_number is set AND already exists for the tenant (partial unique index); return `STAFF_EMPLOYEE_NUMBER_TAKEN`
creating → consume 1 unit of `staff_slot` entitlement against (application_id, tenant_id); refuse with `STAFF_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` when cap reached (Small: 5, Medium: 50, Enterprise: unlimited)
creating → default employment_status='pre_hire' when unset; direct-to-active is allowed but requires the caller to carry `staff.hire` (bypasses the standard candidate-accepted → onboarding flow)

creating → validate started_at is not more than 365 days in the future (rejects nonsense hire dates) and not more than 90 days in the past (rejects retroactive hires without the `staff.hire.retroactive` permission)
creating → refuse when probation_ends_at is set AND probation_ends_at > started_at + 365 days (violates `probation_within_bounds` rule)
creating → refuse when reports_to_staff_id is set AND `staff_org_chart` entitlement is off for the tenant (returns `STAFF_ORG_CHART_NOT_ENTITLED`)
creating → refuse when reports_to_staff_id.tenant_id != tenant_id (cross-tenant FK smuggling)
creating → refuse when reports_to_staff_id.employment_status IN ('offboarding', 'offboarded') (returns `STAFF_MANAGER_NOT_ACTIVE`)

creating → refuse when compensation_amount_cents / compensation_type / compensation_currency / pay_frequency are set AND caller does NOT carry `staff.manage.compensation` (returns `STAFF_COMPENSATION_PERMISSION_REQUIRED`)
creating → refuse when compensation_amount_cents is set AND `staff_compensation_tracking` entitlement is off for the tenant (returns `STAFF_COMPENSATION_TRACKING_NOT_ENABLED` — HR must opt in first)

creating → refuse when is_branch_manager=true AND another active Staff for this branch already has is_branch_manager=true (returns `BRANCH_ALREADY_HAS_MANAGER` — auto-demote is NOT the default; caller must explicitly demote first, or use the /promote-to-manager route which handles the atomic swap in one transaction)
creating → refuse when is_branch_manager=true AND employment_status != 'active' (returns `BRANCH_MANAGER_MUST_BE_ACTIVE` — a pre_hire cannot be a branch manager)
creating → set started_at defaults to today() when unset
creating → fire StaffHired if employment_status='active' (the pre_hire → active transition path fires it on updating instead)
updating user_id → REFUSE (returns `STAFF_USER_ID_IMMUTABLE`). You do not repoint a Staff at a different User; the correct path is offboard + new hire.
updating started_at → REFUSE post-create when employment_status != 'pre_hire' (returns `STAFF_STARTED_AT_LOCKED` — you can adjust the planned hire date only during the pre_hire window)
updating branch_id → refuse in Wave 2b (returns `STAFF_BRANCH_IMMUTABLE_WAVE_2B`). Wave 3+ ships the transfer flow that fires StaffBranchChanged.
updating position → fire StaffPositionChanged
updating department → fire StaffDepartmentChanged
updating employment_status pre_hire → active → fire StaffHired; consume the second half of staff_slot (the first was consumed on create — this branch is a no-op idempotent recheck)
updating employment_status active → on_leave → fire StaffPutOnLeave; cascade-pause linked Coach (if any) via CascadeCoachStatusOnStaffOffboarding listener
updating employment_status on_leave → active → fire StaffReturnedFromLeave; resume linked Coach if paused as a side-effect
updating employment_status active/on_leave → suspended → refuse when suspension_reason is unset (returns `STAFF_SUSPENSION_REASON_REQUIRED`); fire StaffSuspended
updating employment_status suspended → active → fire StaffReinstated

updating employment_status active/on_leave/suspended → offboarding → refuse when termination_reason is unset (returns `STAFF_TERMINATION_REASON_REQUIRED` — no offboard without a documented reason); fire StaffOffboardingStarted; dispatch reminder cascade

updating employment_status offboarding → offboarded → refuse when active TeamMember rows (Wave 3+) reference this Staff (member_type='staff' AND member_id=this) with `STAFF_HAS_TEAM_MEMBERSHIPS` — caller must remove team memberships first

updating employment_status offboarding → offboarded → refuse when active Coach row references this Staff AND coach.status != 'archived' (returns `STAFF_HAS_ACTIVE_COACH_PROFILE` — caller must archive the coach first, or invoke /offboard with cascade=true to archive the coach in the same transaction)
updating employment_status offboarding → offboarded → set ended_at = today() when unset; release 1 unit of staff_slot entitlement; fire StaffOffboarded

updating employment_status offboarded → active (rehire) → refuse when the user already has an active Staff row in the tenant (defense-in-depth against duplicate rehires); consume 1 unit of staff_slot; reset ended_at to null; fire StaffReactivated

updating is_branch_manager false → true → refuse when another active branch_manager exists (returns `BRANCH_ALREADY_HAS_MANAGER`); refuse when employment_status != 'active' (returns `BRANCH_MANAGER_MUST_BE_ACTIVE`); fire StaffPromotedToBranchManager

updating is_branch_manager true → false → fire StaffDemotedFromBranchManager; refuse when this is the last active manager for the branch AND the caller is not explicitly acknowledging the leaderless state (returns `BRANCH_WOULD_HAVE_NO_MANAGER` — the promote-to-manager route handles the atomic swap; the demote-alone route surfaces this warning)

updating reports_to_staff_id → refuse when it forms a cycle (walks the chain; returns `STAFF_ORG_CHART_CYCLE`); refuse when the target is offboarded (returns `STAFF_MANAGER_NOT_ACTIVE`); fire StaffUpdated with changed_fields=['reports_to_staff_id']
updating any compensation_* field → refuse when caller does NOT carry `staff.manage.compensation` (returns `STAFF_COMPENSATION_PERMISSION_REQUIRED`)
updating any compensation_* field → refuse when `staff_compensation_tracking` entitlement is off for the tenant

updating any compensation_* field → refuse when the delta exceeds config('staff.compensation.max_delta_percent', 50) AND caller does NOT carry `staff.manage.compensation.large-delta` (returns `STAFF_COMPENSATION_DELTA_TOO_LARGE` — audit-critical safeguard against payroll fat-finger errors)

updating any compensation_* field → fire CompensationChanged (private-tier event, routes to compensation-audit queue); write to the dedicated `staff_compensation_history` audit table (SEPARATE from the normal `audits` table — this stream has 7-year financial-record retention)

updating emergency_contact_name / emergency_contact_phone / emergency_contact_relationship / tax_id_last4 → refuse when caller does NOT carry `staff.manage.emergency_contact` (returns `STAFF_EMERGENCY_CONTACT_PERMISSION_REQUIRED`); write to the normal audits table (7-year retention for confidential-tier fields)
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when employment_status NOT IN ('offboarded') — must be offboarded first (returns `STAFF_MUST_BE_OFFBOARDED_BEFORE_DELETE`)
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when active Coach row references this Staff AND coach.status != 'archived' (returns `STAFF_HAS_ACTIVE_COACH_PROFILE`)
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when active TeamMember rows reference this Staff (returns `STAFF_HAS_TEAM_MEMBERSHIPS`)

deleting (hard-delete) → RESTRICT while any Coach row references (even soft-deleted ones inside their retention window); RESTRICT while any finance::PayrollLine references (Wave 4); allow only after 7-year financial retention

post-commit invariant recheck → if is_branch_manager=true and the row now conflicts with another true row for the same branch (race caught by the ReconcileBranchManagerInvariantJob), fire a diagnostic event; this should never happen because the observer uses SELECT ... FOR UPDATE inside the transaction, but the reconciler is the belt-and-suspenders
creating → populate ULID prefix `coa_`
creating → refuse when tenant does NOT hold `staff` feature (defense-in-depth)
creating → refuse when Staff.tenant_id != coach.tenant_id (cross-tenant defense-in-depth)

creating → refuse when parent Staff.employment_status != 'active' (returns `COACH_STAFF_NOT_ACTIVE` — enforces the `coach_staff_active` rule; a pre_hire cannot yet be a coach; an offboarded staff cannot regain a coach profile without going through StaffReactivated first)
creating → refuse when parent Staff already has a live (deleted_at IS NULL) Coach row via the unique(staff_id) partial index; return `COACH_STAFF_ALREADY_HAS_COACH_ROW`
creating → consume 1 unit of `coach_slot` entitlement against (application_id, tenant_id); refuse with `COACH_QUOTA_EXCEEDED` when cap reached (Small: 1, Medium: 10, Enterprise: unlimited)

creating → refuse when sport_key is not in the Wave 3 sports registry (skipped when the sports module is not yet booted — Wave 2b treats sport_key as free-text with a soft validation warning; Wave 3 hardens to registry-only)
creating → refuse when coach_started_at < staff.started_at (returns `COACH_STARTED_BEFORE_STAFF` — you cannot have been coaching before your employment began)
creating → refuse when coach_started_at > today() + 90 days (rejects nonsense future dates)
creating → default coach_started_at = staff.started_at when unset

creating → validate certifications array against schemas/coach.schema.json.certifications; refuse when any entry violates structural shape (`valid_certifications_shape` rule); refuse when any entry has expires_at < certified_at
creating → validate additional_sports array — refuse when it contains the primary sport_key (dedup) or an entry not in the sports registry
creating → validate languages array — refuse when any entry is not a valid BCP-47 tag (`valid_bcp47_locale` rule)
creating → validate public_email against `valid_url` rule (email variant)
creating → validate public_phone against `valid_e164_phone` rule when set
creating → validate availability_pattern against `valid_availability_pattern` rule when set
creating → validate rating field is null on create (rating writes are reserved for AggregateCoachRatingsJob)
creating → set status='active' when unset; refuse status='archived' on create (archived is a lifecycle-terminal state, not an initial state)
creating → fire CoachCreated
updating staff_id → REFUSE (returns `COACH_STAFF_ID_IMMUTABLE`). A Coach row belongs to one Staff for its entire lifetime.
updating sport_key → fire CoachSportChanged; invalidate the sport-directory cache
updating certifications → diff old vs new; fire CoachCertificationAdded per added entry; fire CoachCertificationRemoved per removed entry; refuse when a modified entry violates the structural shape

updating certifications → when `coach_certifications_verified` entitlement is on (Enterprise), new entries land in `pending_verification` state (added `verified_at: null` to the entry object); HR must invoke the verify action to promote to verified

updating rating → REFUSE writes from any caller EXCEPT the AggregateCoachRatingsJob (checked via `Bus::isDispatchedFromJob(AggregateCoachRatingsJob::class)` or similar). Returns `COACH_RATING_WRITE_FORBIDDEN`
updating status active → paused → fire CoachStatusChanged; cascade — pause future SessionBookings referencing this coach (Wave 3+; skipped until session module lands)
updating status paused → active → fire CoachStatusChanged

updating status active/paused → archived → refuse when active TeamMember rows reference this Coach (member_type='coach', member_id=this) with `COACH_HAS_TEAM_MEMBERSHIPS` — caller must remove team memberships first; set coach_ended_at = today() when unset; fire CoachStatusChanged; release 1 unit of coach_slot entitlement
updating availability_pattern → validate via `valid_availability_pattern` rule; fire CoachAvailabilityChanged

updating public_email / public_phone / bio / head_shot_url / languages → fire CoachStatusChanged only when status changed (these fields are self-service via the coach's own /me/coach endpoint; the coach edits their public-facing bio + contact)
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when status != 'archived' (returns `COACH_MUST_BE_ARCHIVED_BEFORE_DELETE`)
deleting (soft-delete) → refuse when active TeamMember rows reference (returns `COACH_HAS_TEAM_MEMBERSHIPS`)
deleting (hard-delete) → RESTRICT while any finance::PayrollLine.coach_commission_id references (Wave 4); allow only after 7-year financial retention
